channel.title: ThreatDigest Hub - Curated Cyber Incidents
channel.description: A curated list of recent cyber incidents, attacks, and security threats.

item.title: [DARK WEB] akira ransomware: new victim 'Stokes'

item.description:
Discovery Date: 2026-03-09
In response to the ongoing brutal crimes of the occupying regime against the people of the resistanc...

item.title: Ransomware incident responder gave info to BlackCat cybercriminals during negotiations, DOJ alleges
item.description: The Justice Department is accusing an incident responder of conducting cyberattacks and helping ransomware gangs negotiate higher payouts from the same victims he was working for. Angelo Martino surrendered to the U.S. Marshals on Tuesday and bonded out the same day, agreeing to perform no cyber industry work as part of his release.

item.title: Watch out for fake Malwarebytes renewal notices in your calendar
item.description: We’ve become aware of a scam campaign sending fake calendar invites that impersonate Malwarebytes and attempt to trick recipients into calling a scam “billing support” number. We have written before about how calendar invites can be abused for phishing, and even about how Google Calendar invites can be weaponized to steal private data. The amounts in these fake invites are large and attention-grabbing, usually several hundred dollars for multiple years of service.

item.title: INTERPOL Dismantles 45,000 Malicious IPs, Arrests 94 in Global Cybercrime
item.description: INTERPOL on Friday announced the takedown of 45,000 malicious IP addresses and servers used in connection with phishing, malware, and ransomware campaigns, as part of the agency's ongoing efforts to dismantle criminal networks, disrupt emerging threats, and safeguard victims from scams. The effort is part of an international law enforcement operation that involved 72 countries and territories. It also led to the arrest of 94 people, with another 110 individuals still under investigation.

item.title: Project 0 pledges refunds after GitHub compromise triggers phishing attack on DeFi users
Cyber Attack Raises Fears of Supply Chain Targets in Iran War
item.description: Highlighting the potential impact on global supply chains from the U.S.-Israel war against Iran, an Iran-linked group said it hacked the Stryker Corporation, which makes medical devices and is based in Michigan. Handala, a hacker group, said the attack caused “global disruption” to its systems, and was in retaliation for the bombing of the Minab school in Iran. The Guardian reports that the attack is seen as widening the Middle East conflict into the cyber realm.
